capabilities.definition: Border Control Function for ingress/egress of NG911 SIP traffic.

capabilities.name: i3 event logging
capabilities.definition: Log NG911 i3 functional-element events for audit and troubleshooting.

capabilities.name: PSAP failover / overflow
capabilities.definition: Failover and overflow of emergency calls to alternate PSAPs.

capabilities.name: Misdial / non-emergency filter
capabilities.definition: Filter or redirect obvious misdials and non-emergency contacts where policy allows.

capabilities.name: Telematics / ACN to 911
capabilities.definition: Receive automatic crash notification / vehicle telematics into the PSAP.

capabilities.name: NG911–CAD call handoff
capabilities.definition: Hand off NG911 call/incident data into CAD (deep CAD remains separate vertical).

capabilities.name: Multi-line / multi-agency answer
capabilities.definition: Support multi-line answering and multi-agency call taking positions.

capabilities.name: VMS search / retrieve
capabilities.definition: Search and retrieve recorded video by time, camera, and incident.

capabilities.name: Video retention policy
capabilities.definition: Enforce retention, hold, and purge policies for agency video evidence.

capabilities.name: PTZ camera control
capabilities.definition: Pan-tilt-zoom control of fixed CCTV cameras from ops workstations.

capabilities.name: Video wall / ops display
capabilities.definition: Display multi-camera layouts on ops/EOC video walls.

capabilities.name: Body-worn evidence workflow
capabilities.definition: Upload, classify, and chain-of-custody body-worn camera evidence.

capabilities.name: Livestream to PSAP / CAD
capabilities.definition: Stream live video into PSAP/CAD/EOC for situational awareness.

capabilities.name: ALPR hotlist matching
capabilities.definition: Match ALPR reads against hotlists and alert ops in near real time.

capabilities.name: Gunshot → CAD incident
capabilities.definition: Create or enrich CAD incidents from gunshot detection alerts.

capabilities.name: Sensor alert routing
capabilities.definition: Route IoT/sensor alerts to the correct PSAP/ops queue by policy.

capabilities.name: Traffic / roadway sensors
capabilities.definition: Ingest traffic and roadway sensors for incident awareness.

capabilities.name: UAS video downlink
capabilities.definition: Receive and display drone video downlink in ops/CAD workflows.

capabilities.name: UAS geo-fence / airspace
capabilities.definition: Enforce geo-fence and airspace constraints for dispatched UAS.

capabilities.name: Video analytics detection
capabilities.definition: Video analytics for object/person/vehicle detection feeding alerts.

capabilities.name: Multi-sensor fusion
capabilities.definition: Correlate multi-sensor events (video, ALPR, gunshot) into one picture.

capabilities.name: Video export / redaction
capabilities.definition: Export and redact video for discovery, FOIA, and court packages.

capabilities.name: CIT / mental-health flags
capabilities.definition: Flag incidents and subjects for CIT / mental-health response protocols.

capabilities.name: Juvenile subject flags
capabilities.definition: Flag juvenile subjects and apply age-sensitive handling notes in CAD.

capabilities.name: Geo-fence / zone alerts
capabilities.definition: Alert dispatch when units or incidents enter configured geo-fences or zones.

capabilities.name: Incident clone / split
capabilities.definition: Clone or split incidents while preserving linked history and parties.

capabilities.name: Supervisor override
capabilities.definition: Allow authorized supervisors to override priority, assignment, or SOP locks.

capabilities.name: Jail / court interface touch
capabilities.definition: Interface CAD bookings and court appearance touchpoints with jail/court systems.

capabilities.name: Weather / road closure layers
capabilities.definition: Display weather and road-closure layers affecting response routing.

capabilities.name: Skill-based unit recommendation
capabilities.definition: Recommend units by certified skills (e.g. CIT, K9, bilingual) in addition to proximity.

capabilities.name: Scheduled / planned events
capabilities.definition: Create and manage scheduled or planned events with pre-assigned resources.

capabilities.name: Quarantine / information hold
capabilities.definition: Place sensitive incidents on quarantine or information-hold with restricted visibility.

capabilities.name: Dispatcher shift handoff
capabilities.definition: Structured dispatcher shift handoff of active incidents and pending work.

capabilities.name: Person-of-interest alerts
capabilities.definition: Alert when a person of interest or watchlist hit attaches to an incident or query.

capabilities.name: Location discrepancy handling
capabilities.definition: Detect and resolve discrepancies between reported, ALI/AML, and verified locations.

capabilities.name: Silent-call protocol
capabilities.definition: Apply silent/open-line call protocols including staged verification steps.

capabilities.name: RTT / text transfer
capabilities.definition: Transfer real-time text (RTT) and text sessions between PSAPs without dropping media.

capabilities.name: Multimedia retention policy
capabilities.definition: Retain NG911 multimedia (image/video) per agency retention and legal hold policy.

capabilities.name: ESRP congestion / alternate route
capabilities.definition: Handle ESRP congestion with alternate routing and overflow policies.

capabilities.name: Legacy selective-router bridge
capabilities.definition: Bridge legacy selective-router / E911 trunks into the NG911 i3 call path.

capabilities.name: Orphan / stranded call recovery
capabilities.definition: Recover orphaned or stranded emergency sessions after network or FE failure.

capabilities.name: Poison control / specialty transfer
capabilities.definition: Transfer emergency callers to poison control or other specialty advice lines with location retained.

capabilities.name: BCF TLS / SRTP media security
capabilities.definition: Enforce TLS signaling and SRTP media security at the Border Control Function.

capabilities.name: Abandoned-call callback policy
capabilities.definition: Apply configurable abandoned-call callback policy (attempts, timers, disposition).

capabilities.name: Mobile incident capture
capabilities.definition: Capture and update incident details from a field mobile application.

capabilities.name: Field narrative / forms
capabilities.definition: Complete structured field narratives and electronic forms linked to incidents.

capabilities.name: Field offline sync
capabilities.definition: Operate field apps offline and synchronize when connectivity returns.

capabilities.name: Field digital evidence capture
capabilities.definition: Capture photos, video, and files from the field device into the evidence workflow.

capabilities.name: Field person / vehicle identification
capabilities.definition: Query and attach person/vehicle identification results from the field app.

capabilities.name: eCitation / electronic citation
capabilities.definition: Issue electronic citations from the field and link them to the incident/case.

capabilities.name: Field supervisor approval
capabilities.definition: Allow field supervisors to review and approve reports or evidence packages.

capabilities.name: Field–CAD status push
capabilities.definition: Push unit and incident status updates from the field app into CAD.

capabilities.name: Field–BWC upload trigger
capabilities.definition: Trigger or confirm body-worn camera media upload from the field workflow.

capabilities.name: RMS case / incident packaging
capabilities.definition: Package case and incident records for prosecution, records, and sharing.

capabilities.name: Evidence / property room link
capabilities.definition: Link RMS cases to evidence and property-room custody records.

capabilities.name: RMS supplemental reports
capabilities.definition: Create and manage supplemental / follow-up reports on an incident case.

capabilities.name: RMS retention / legal hold
capabilities.definition: Apply retention schedules and legal holds to RMS records and evidence metadata.

capabilities.name: EOC common operating picture
capabilities.definition: Present a multi-source common operating picture for EOC / C2 coordination.

capabilities.name: MCX affiliation / group management
capabilities.definition: Affiliate users/devices to MCX groups and manage membership.

capabilities.name: MCPTT private call
capabilities.definition: Support MCPTT private (one-to-one) calls between authorized users.

capabilities.name: MCPTT floor control
capabilities.definition: Floor control / talker arbitration for MCPTT group calls.

capabilities.name: MCX location reporting
capabilities.definition: Report and display MCX user/device location to authorized consumers.

capabilities.name: MCData short data / SDS
capabilities.definition: Send and receive MCData short data service (SDS) messages.

capabilities.name: MCData file distribution
capabilities.definition: Distribute files over MCData to groups or individuals.

capabilities.name: MCVideo pull / remote view
capabilities.definition: Pull or remotely view MCVideo streams for authorized supervisors.

capabilities.name: MCX priority / preemption
capabilities.definition: Apply MCX priority levels and preemption policies for critical traffic.

capabilities.name: MCX off-network / ProSe touch
capabilities.definition: Support off-network / proximity services touchpoints where specified.

capabilities.name: MCX user authentication
capabilities.definition: Authenticate MCX users and bind identities to agency directories.

capabilities.name: MCX call recording
capabilities.definition: Record MCPTT/MCVideo sessions with retention controls.

capabilities.name: MCX multi-agency talkgroups
capabilities.definition: Support multi-agency MCX talkgroups and inter-agency affiliation.

capabilities.name: Geo-targeted public alerts
capabilities.definition: Originate geo-targeted public warning alerts to selected areas.

capabilities.name: CAP alert relay
capabilities.definition: Relay Common Alerting Protocol (CAP) messages to downstream channels.

capabilities.name: GIS layer management
capabilities.definition: Manage and toggle operational GIS layers for dispatch and EOC maps.

capabilities.name: Geocode / reverse geocode
capabilities.definition: Geocode addresses and reverse-geocode coordinates for incident location.

capabilities.name: Response routing / ETA
capabilities.definition: Compute response routes and ETA using GIS network data.

capabilities.name: WEA / wireless emergency alert touch
capabilities.definition: Touchpoint to originate or relay Wireless Emergency Alerts (WEA).
